content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as recursive self-improving agents themselves, where the memory architecture actively selects which new agent interaction traces to store, prune, or compress based on intrinsic curiosity signals, rather than being a passive storage layer for a separate control agent. This turns the traditional agent-memory hierarchy into a dual-agent system where a curiosity-driven memory agent collaborates with the main task agent to boost overall performance.
